--- Thread (50 messages, 2018-06-05) ---
[2018-06-05 02:08] cogito3 (Cogito): floating 2 gold is not ideal but is not terrible, it's floating 3+ that you (typically) want to avoid
[2018-06-05 02:08] namington: 2 tarsiers vs 1 tarsier
[2018-06-05 02:09] cogito3 (Cogito): DD/DC is usually a bad build b/c you don't want to cut a drone for green units typically, but it is the standard zemora line
[2018-06-05 02:10] willflame: so in a situation where you would float 3+ gold, would you repurpose the tech or buy engineers?
[2018-06-05 02:10] cogito3 (Cogito): depends on the situation lol. I guess usually the former if you can, b/c 2 gold for 1 defense isn't the best, but sometimes you do need the engis
[2018-06-05 02:10] namington: if the engineers wont matter the next turn, its strictly worse than saving it up
[2018-06-05 02:10] amalloy: depends. you might float the gold anyway. prismata doesn't lend itself well to very specific answers to very specific questions
[2018-06-05 02:10] namington: if the engineers WILL matter, then yeah, buy engineers
[2018-06-05 02:11] namington: unless youre saving up for something specific
[2018-06-05 02:11] namington: like lucina + a wall for example
[2018-06-05 02:11] cogito3 (Cogito): if the question is specific to an individual situation we can give specific answers
[2018-06-05 02:11] cogito3 (Cogito): right, occasionally you do float the gold for a specific purpose the following turn
[2018-06-05 02:11] willflame: ok, i'm just looking for ideas because i get into a lot of situations where i don't know what to do and end up in a really bad position
[2018-06-05 02:12] willflame: thanks for all the help so far though
[2018-06-05 02:12] cogito3 (Cogito): yeah, the only real cure for that is experience, but you can post replays/screenshots of particular situations where you run into trouble and ppl can help you out better
[2018-06-05 02:12] amalloy: practice will help. in general, try to find a *reason* to buy or not buy a specific thing
[2018-06-05 02:12] amalloy: "i'm buying this engineer because __________"
[2018-06-05 02:13] cogito3 (Cogito): another thing to note is if you're consistently floating a lot of gold, that might mean you're underteched
[2018-06-05 02:13] namington: having 2-4 gold left over is typical for mid-high econ games, especially if theyre not super tech heavy
[2018-06-05 02:14] namington: or if they have good tech sinks like pixies
[2018-06-05 02:14] cogito3 (Cogito): umm...2 gold yeah, 3-4 not really
[2018-06-05 02:14] namington: 3-4 happens but you'd usually buy engineers with it
[2018-06-05 02:14] namington: at the point where youre floating that much
[2018-06-05 02:14] namington: cause the defense matters.
[2018-06-05 02:15] cogito3 (Cogito): true, though I think if you have more than 3 engis at once something probably went wrong
[2018-06-05 02:15] cogito3 (Cogito): as always it does depend on the situation
[2018-06-05 02:15] namington: well theres some cases where engis are literally the best soak so you just shift e with like 8 gold left over
[2018-06-05 02:15] namington: thats not super common tho
[2018-06-05 02:15] namington: only happens if like, walls are out of supply and forcefields are still a bad idea
[2018-06-05 02:15] cogito3 (Cogito): when is that? If you have no B?
[2018-06-05 02:16] cogito3 (Cogito): well if wall supply is out I think inflation is prob high enough you need the forcefields
[2018-06-05 02:16] namington: perhaps I should say, you cant buy enough forcefields to fit your needs
[2018-06-05 02:16] namington: thats _sometimes_ indicative of being underteched tho
[2018-06-05 02:16] cogito3 (Cogito): yeah, I think most of the time you want another conduit in that situation
[2018-06-05 02:16] namington: how many conduits you want late-game is  a rly tough question though
[2018-06-05 02:17] amalloy: that reminds me, i never know when to buy a conduit just for forcefields. it never feels right to spend 4 on a conduit so that i can later forcefield for 2 when i could just buy 2 engis instead
[2018-06-05 02:17] cogito3 (Cogito): most of the time you don't want it *just* for forcefields
[2018-06-05 02:18] willflame: so is it also not correct to buy a second blastforge just for Walls?
[2018-06-05 02:18] cogito3 (Cogito): yes (obligatory "for the most part" disclaimer)
[2018-06-05 02:18] amalloy: @WillFlame generally a second blastforge specifically to get two walls per turn is wrong
[2018-06-05 02:18] nvlx (Fate): 2 forges for walls is prertty miserable
[2018-06-05 02:18] namington: just for walls, usually yeah
[2018-06-05 02:18] nvlx (Fate): because your wall supply will run out fast
[2018-06-05 02:18] namington: walls arent even that efficient soak-wise and
[2018-06-05 02:19] amalloy: but like, if you're spending the blue on something else and want walls as well, sure
[2018-06-05 02:19] namington: supply is a huge concern
[2018-06-05 02:19] cogito3 (Cogito): though note that conduit+forcefield becomes cheaper than engis after the 2nd forcefield
[2018-06-05 02:19] namington: its not like aegis where the sheer efficiency of the soak makes it worth it
[2018-06-05 02:19] willflame: yeah, i've been doing that pretty often :/
[2018-06-05 02:19] willflame: is it worth going tri-colour when the only blue random set unit requires two blue?

[2018-06-05 02:20] amalloy: question seems way too vague
[2018-06-05 02:20] cogito3 (Cogito): and what are the other units? :p
[2018-06-05 02:20] cogito3 (Cogito): that one really depends on the set, matrix is good enough it's sometimes worth it to go BB just for it, but again, that depends on the other units
[2018-06-05 02:20] willflame: generally cheap rush-y units
[2018-06-05 02:21] cogito3 (Cogito): if it's a rush set you probably don't have the leeway to get two forges
[2018-06-05 02:21] cogito3 (Cogito): but to emphasize, probably =/= always
[2018-06-05 02:21] amalloy: @WillFlame i think you will get more more useful answers if you mention a specific set
[2018-06-05 02:21] amalloy: you want to know general rules of thumb, but it's better to derive that from a number of specific answers than to ask one general question and try to sift through the clouds of fog
[2018-06-05 02:22] cogito3 (Cogito): agreed. You may find it useful to read through 307th's set reading posts if you haven't already
[2018-06-05 02:22] willflame: i'll take a look through some of my old replays
[2018-06-05 02:25] willflame: i ended up getting a second Blastforge for the Energy Matrix but i fell really far behind in attack
[2018-06-05 02:26] willflame: should i have Centrifuge'd one or not gone blue at all like the bot?
[2018-06-05 02:27] cogito3 (Cogito): centrifuge for the matrix is def a lot better than double forge in this set
[2018-06-05 02:28] cogito3 (Cogito): I personally would've rushed scorches, but that's at least partly a style thing
[2018-06-05 02:28] amalloy: i think scorches are just too good here, and especially you can deny matrix absorb
[2018-06-05 02:29] cogito3 (Cogito): yeah true, scorch strategy might just be better than the fuge-->matrix strategy
[2018-06-05 02:29] amalloy: gauss charge syncs too
[2018-06-05 02:30] willflame: in that game, my opponent actually went for the Omega Splitter and i didn't, but i still lost
[2018-06-05 02:30] cogito3 (Cogito): (btw, the scorch rush line is DD/DC/DA/buy 2 scorches)
[2018-06-05 02:30] willflame: i'll keep that in mind, thanks!
[2018-06-05 02:31] cogito3 (Cogito): p2 gets DD/DDC/DA/11 (where 1 is scorch)
[2018-06-05 02:32] cogito3 (Cogito): in the 2nd game you posted, you didn't pressure the 3rd engi line enough, you should've went DA after they did DDE
[2018-06-05 02:32] cogito3 (Cogito): (also, TT into smorcus is a lot better than smorcus into TT. In the latter smorcus gets absorbed twice, in the former they get no absorb until you have all 3 damage out)
[2018-06-05 02:33] amalloy: @Cogito not DA, but vivid A
[2018-06-05 02:33] cogito3 (Cogito): oh right, forgot vivid is in the set
[2018-06-05 02:34] willflame: i also don't know what to do when there are drone alternatives in the set
[2018-06-05 02:34] amalloy: conveniently, 307th just wrote an article about that
[2018-06-05 02:34] cogito3 (Cogito): link: https://prismatalibrary.blog/2018/06/03/alternate-drones/
Alternate Drones
Alternate drones are very difficult units to use if you aren’t familiar with them. They introduce a bunch of new possible openings, and you often want to be using them even on t…
[2018-06-05 02:34] willflame: oh cool, i didn't notice that one
[2018-06-05 02:35] cogito3 (Cogito): the basic strat with vivid is, if you would only buy 1 drone anyway, buy vivid instead
[2018-06-05 02:36] cogito3 (Cogito): I will say in the 2nd game you posted, I think the green/red strat should beat the omega strat if you get vivid/animus turn 2. (There may even be a way to fit endo kit into the build)
[2018-06-05 02:39] namington: as a hypothetical, is there ever a situation where p1 opener 1. DD 2. AE isnt utterly terrible?
[2018-06-05 02:39] namington: I cant think of like, any possible situation in which its even passable
[2018-06-05 02:40] cogito3 (Cogito): I think it would require p2 going turn 1 animus
[2018-06-05 02:40] namington: yeah thats what i thought, or like
[2018-06-05 02:40] cogito3 (Cogito): in like a vore/botch set
[2018-06-05 02:40] cogito3 (Cogito): they can only get 1 vore from manimus though, so you won't need the engi yet
[2018-06-05 02:40] cogito3 (Cogito): but if they get turn 1 animus, you'll need to replace the two engis they kill
[2018-06-05 02:41] cogito3 (Cogito): so yeah, I guess it's possible
[2018-06-05 02:41] namington: isnt that just strictly worse than engiing t3
[2018-06-05 02:41] cogito3 (Cogito): DD/A/A/11 (where 1 is vore or botch)
[2018-06-05 02:41] cogito3 (Cogito): yeah I guess you can wait a turn
[2018-06-05 02:42] cogito3 (Cogito): mayybe if there's also galvani in the set
[2018-06-05 02:42] mqp: namington if there was vivid and galvani and antima and smorcus it could be not insane to get DD/AE/123
[2018-06-05 02:42] namington: yeah i was thinking some weird galvani set but
[2018-06-05 02:42] cogito3 (Cogito): that's a funny strat, I can see it working
[2018-06-05 02:42] namington: you need vivids for it to be worth it
[2018-06-05 02:42] namington: I dont think id ever spot that line in a real game
[2018-06-05 02:43] cogito3 (Cogito): though is it better than A1 on turn 2
[2018-06-05 02:43] cogito3 (Cogito): then turn 3 you get smorcus/galvani

[2018-06-05 03:56] ruinedshadows: you'll get lots of mentors that way
[2018-06-05 03:56] Deleted User (Deleted User): I may try to set something up in that case
[2018-06-05 03:56] ruinedshadows: but I agree, it really is a good way to learn the game
[2018-06-05 03:56] ruinedshadows: I'd love to volunteer
[2018-06-05 03:57] ruinedshadows: not that I'm good but I can certainly help out beginning players
[2018-06-05 03:57] ruinedshadows: I love teaching and learning about the game
[2018-06-05 03:57] Deleted User (Deleted User): I'm not the best teacher myself, but i cringe at bad plays a lot so i love maing bad players make good plays
[2018-06-05 03:57] Deleted User (Deleted User): There are a lot of simple things about the game that make a lot of sense once you know about them
[2018-06-05 03:58] Deleted User (Deleted User): But are hard to come up with yourself
[2018-06-05 03:58] Deleted User (Deleted User): eg. "Doomed drone is not a worse drone, its a better engineer"
[2018-06-05 03:59] ruinedshadows: and even more general concepts like the role of absorb and how to deny absorb
[2018-06-05 03:59] ruinedshadows: like some early players wonder why it would ever be good to "waste" attack to buy units
[2018-06-05 03:59] ruinedshadows: or why have a two buildtime unit can actually be good
[2018-06-05 04:00] camzace: newer players need to be taught that two buildtime can be a significant cost
[2018-06-05 04:00] camzace: like late game tarsiers
[2018-06-05 04:00] ruinedshadows: and late drones are bad
[2018-06-05 04:01] Deleted User (Deleted User): How many people actually frequent the prismata subreddit?
[2018-06-05 04:02] masn6811 (Masne98888): as cooledcannon mentioned, we -have- a mentor system at MasN hub
[2018-06-05 04:02] masn6811 (Masne98888): people just don't use it much, but it's there
[2018-06-05 04:03] masn6811 (Masne98888): for those of us with a mic, there's a voice channel there, if not, there's a text channel that information can be written in
[2018-06-05 04:03] masn6811 (Masne98888): the prismata client has a `/join` command, but that's not preferred because a lot of concepts are useful to refer back to later
[2018-06-05 04:03] masn6811 (Masne98888): so that's why we have a discord room
[2018-06-05 04:04] amalloy: @ruinedshadows i mean, a bt2 unit is strictly worse than the same unit with bt1, so it's not quite right to say that b2 is actually good. rather, there are circumstances in which the downside of bt2 matters less than usual

messages.author: ruinedshadows
